title: Old Money

jokes.text: Oh, what could be more fun than today's trip to the liquor store?
jokes.setup: Homer suggests they'll do something more fun next time
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically implies a liquor store trip was fun for the kids
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - good character integration and economy

jokes.text: Grampa smells like that trunk with the wet bottom
jokes.setup: Kids commenting on Grampa's smell
jokes.punchline: Specific, weird comparison to a trunk with wet bottom
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific and memorable comparison - good visual imagery

jokes.text: He smells like a photo lab
jokes.setup: Continuing the smell comparisons
jokes.punchline: Another specific, unexpected smell comparison
jokes.explanation: Good specific comparison, works as part of the escalating sequence

jokes.text: Grampa smells like an old man, which is like a hospital hallway
jokes.setup: The obvious comparison followed by elaboration
jokes.punchline: Extends the obvious into another specific comparison
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - starts obvious then gets specific

jokes.text: My God, you're right!
jokes.setup: Marge says they'll be old someday
jokes.punchline: Homer's shocked realization of obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - treating obvious fact like revelation

jokes.text: Well....
jokes.setup: Homer asks if kids will put him in a home
jokes.punchline: Kids hesitate instead of immediately saying no
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pause - the silence is the joke. Maximum comedy with minimal setup

jokes.text: Series of rejected activity suggestions
jokes.setup: Family brainstorming fun activities for Grampa
jokes.punchline: Each suggestion gets shot down for age-appropriate reasons
jokes.explanation: Good escalating sequence but somewhat predictable structure

jokes.text: Like I'm gonna ruin $600 teeth on 40 cents' worth of beef
jokes.setup: Discount Lion Safari suggestion
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical concern about cheap safari meat vs expensive dental work
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific joke with perfect economic structure and quotable phrasing

jokes.text: It's Simpson, and these aren't my pills
jokes.setup: Nurse calls him Mr. Simmons and dismisses his complaint
jokes.punchline: Grampa corrects both the name AND reasserts his original point
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Grampa's persistence and clarity

jokes.text: Excuse me. My name is Simmons. I have the wrong pills
jokes.setup: Just after Grampa corrected the name mistake
jokes.punchline: Bea makes the same name mistake while having the same problem
jokes.explanation: Nice comedic coincidence that sets up the meet-cute

jokes.text: and two of the bluest eyes I've ever seen in my life
jokes.setup: Bea listing her pills - red for back, yellow for arrhythmia
jokes.punchline: Switches from describing pills to describing Grampa's eyes
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection with perfect romantic setup - excellent craft

jokes.text: Look at us. We're staring like a couple of stupid punk teenagers
jokes.setup: Grampa and Bea having a moment of mutual attraction
jokes.punchline: Grampa's self-aware observation about their behavior
jokes.explanation: Sweet self-awareness with good character voice

jokes.text: Oh, I wasn't staring, it's, uh, my lazy eye
jokes.setup: Grampa calls out their mutual staring
jokes.punchline: Bea's transparent excuse
jokes.explanation: Charming elderly flirtation with good character chemistry

jokes.text: Care to tip the wrist with me?
jokes.setup: Grampa asking Bea out for drinks
jokes.punchline: Old-fashioned euphemism for drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect period slang for the character - good authenticity

jokes.text: Widower, one son, one working kidney
jokes.setup: Bea asks him to tell her about himself
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact listing that gets increasingly personal/medical
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from social to medical details - very economical

jokes.text: Widowed, bad hip, liver disorder. You left something out: Ravishing!
jokes.setup: Following Grampa's medical/personal inventory pattern
jokes.punchline: Switches from medical problems to romantic compliment
jokes.explanation: Great callback to his format with romantic twist - well-structured

jokes.text: You'd think this would get easier!
jokes.setup: Grampa struggling to ask Bea out despite their obvious mutual interest
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on dating being hard even when you're old
jokes.explanation: Universal truth about dating awkwardness - good relatability

jokes.text: Never fly solo again
jokes.setup: Grampa looking for pomade to get ready
jokes.punchline: Hair product slogan that's ironically appropriate for his new romance
jokes.explanation: Clever product placement that comments on the story

jokes.text: Oh, hello, young lady. Is your grandmother home?
jokes.setup: Grampa arriving at Bea's door
jokes.punchline: Assuming someone else answered instead of recognizing dressed-up Bea
jokes.explanation: Classic elderly romance trope executed well

jokes.text: I'd better keep my good eye on you. Damn straight.
jokes.setup: Reference back to Bea's earlier 'lazy eye' excuse
jokes.punchline: Bea acknowledging the flirtation, Grampa's enthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Good callback with romantic payoff

jokes.text: The battleship New Jersey. You idiot! My girlfriend, Bea.
jokes.setup: Herman asks about the special lady having a birthday
jokes.punchline: Herman assumes it's a ship, not a person
jokes.explanation: Good character-based misunderstanding for military antique dealer

jokes.text: Nothing says 'I love you' better than a military antique
jokes.setup: Grampa looking for a gift for his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Herman's bizarrely specific and inappropriate gift advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific absurdity - Herman would definitely think this

jokes.text: Not the famous one. He wore it for a week in 1796. Before he beat the Sardinians.
jokes.setup: Grampa questions if it's really Napoleon's hat
jokes.punchline: Overly specific and underwhelming provenance story
jokes.explanation: Great specificity makes the fake story funnier

jokes.text: $400. I'll give you $5. Not an offer you should make to a man with a gun.
jokes.setup: Grampa lowballing the Napoleon hat price
jokes.punchline: Herman's threatening response about having weapons
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with character-appropriate threat

jokes.text: She can come with us. There's room for all your friends.
jokes.setup: Grampa mentions his girlfriend Bea
jokes.punchline: Homer's condescending assumption that all Grampa's friends are imaginary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness about his father's social life

jokes.text: She's not invisible, you idiot!
jokes.setup: Homer's condescending comment about imaginary friends
jokes.punchline: Grampa's exasperated correction
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamics showing generational disconnect

jokes.text: Damn these childproof doors!
jokes.setup: Grampa trying to escape from the car
jokes.punchline: Can't open the safety locks meant to keep kids in
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with age-appropriate frustration

jokes.text: That'll be 18.50, Bwana
jokes.setup: Arriving at Discount Lion Safari
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate safari roleplay at discount attraction
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of tourist trap theming

jokes.text: Are we in Africa yet?
jokes.setup: Family driving through discount safari
jokes.punchline: Bart's naive question about the fake safari location
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic about the fake safari experience

jokes.text: Let 'em sleep on their own time!
jokes.setup: Animals sleeping during the day at safari
jokes.punchline: Homer treating wild animals like lazy employees
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - applying work logic to nature. Excellent economy and character voice

jokes.text: What a wingding. This is much better than my girlfriend's party.
jokes.setup: Family stuck in discount safari
jokes.punchline: Grampa's sarcastic comment while missing Bea's birthday
jokes.explanation: Sets up the tragic turn - Grampa's frustration becomes poignant

jokes.text: I got a date with an angel. You don't know how right you are.
jokes.setup: Grampa excited about his date with Bea
jokes.punchline: Jasper's ominous response foreshadowing Bea's death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark foreshadowing disguised as casual conversation

jokes.text: They may say she died of a burst ventricle, but I know she died of a broken heart.
jokes.setup: Jasper explains Bea's medical cause of death
jokes.punchline: Grampa's romantic interpretation of cardiac failure
jokes.explanation: Sweet romantic interpretation with underlying sadness

jokes.text: Is someone talking? I didn't hear anything. Oh, no! Dad's lost his hearing! No, you idiot! I'm ignoring you!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to apologize to Grampa
jokes.punchline: Fake hearing loss revealed as deliberate snubbing
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with family dynamics payoff

jokes.text: There is one catch. You must spend one night in a haunted house. Just kidding.
jokes.setup: Hutz about to reveal inheritance conditions
jokes.punchline: Fake horror movie trope immediately dismissed
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about inheritance movie clichés

jokes.text: Here's a pen with my phone number on it. It looks like a cigar. Isn't that neat?
jokes.setup: Hutz offering legal services
jokes.punchline: Proudly showing off novelty pen at inappropriate moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hutz character moment - tacky and inappropriately timed

jokes.text: I just had to tell you that you're not getting one thin dime!
jokes.setup: Grampa calls Homer to share good news about inheritance
jokes.punchline: Revelation that the good news is Homer's disinheritance
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - calls with good news that's actually punishment

jokes.text: There are rubdowns, and then there are rubdowns.
jokes.setup: Administrator explaining how money makes a difference
jokes.punchline: Suggestive distinction between service levels
jokes.explanation: Relies on innuendo but fits the sleazy character

jokes.text: Yes, but it passes.
jokes.setup: Grampa asks if he ever thinks elderly should be treated humanely regardless of money
jokes.punchline: Administrator's casual admission of temporary empathy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - perfect economy and brutal honesty about healthcare

jokes.text: Take me someplace fun! You're the boss. Next stop, Funsville.
jokes.setup: Grampa wants to enjoy his new wealth
jokes.punchline: Driver's literal interpretation with made-up destination
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor with good character interaction

jokes.text: I'm haunting a family in Texas.
jokes.setup: Bea's ghost appearing to Grampa
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of other haunting commitments
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on ghost logistics

jokes.text: Make other people as happy as you made me.
jokes.setup: Grampa unhappy despite his wealth
jokes.punchline: Bea's advice to share the wealth
jokes.explanation: More plot advancement than comedy, but sweet character moment

jokes.text: Oh, I miss him too, the big fat dickens.
jokes.setup: Bea telling Grampa that Homer misses him
jokes.punchline: Her endearing but honest description of Homer
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment. 'Big fat dickens' is affectionately insulting in an old-fashioned way.

jokes.text: Hey, Bea, I've got to ask you, what was death like? Not as scary as this!
jokes.setup: Grampa asking about the death experience
jokes.punchline: Bea scared by something visual we can infer
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup - death less scary than whatever's happening on screen

jokes.text: Choose your corncob. En garde! Challenge accepted!
jokes.setup: Family dinner scene
jokes.punchline: Kids turning corn eating into a duel
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic making dinner dramatic

jokes.text: All my precious sacks of gold couldn't buy me the joys of a family meal.
jokes.setup: Grampa reflecting on wealth vs family
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top phrasing about simple family dinner
jokes.explanation: Good character voice with melodramatic phrasing

jokes.text: Give it to us. Bart! Forgive him. He's just a stupid little kid who says the first thing in his head. You know, he is wise.
jokes.setup: Grampa announcing he'll give money to deserving people
jokes.punchline: Homer's attempt to criticize Bart backfires into agreement
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic failure - accidentally validates Bart's greed

jokes.text: Not since my marriage to the weather lady has this town been so consumed with rumor and innuendo.
jokes.setup: News report about Grampa's inheritance announcement
jokes.punchline: Kent's personal reference to his own scandalous past
jokes.explanation: Good Kent Brockman character detail - inappropriate personal sharing

jokes.text: Here's a rendering. Note the cobra wrapped around the naked chick.
jokes.setup: Otto pitching his school bus customization plan
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate artwork for school bus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto - completely missing appropriate boundaries for school property

jokes.text: Ownership is divided-- What are you doing? I dread the day when $100,000 isn't worth groveling for.
jokes.setup: Burns begging for Grampa's money
jokes.punchline: Burns' twisted perspective on wealth and dignity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line - reveals his warped values about money and pride

jokes.text: It's pretty stupid, but so far you're the front-runner.
jokes.setup: Barney pitching his treasure map idea
jokes.punchline: Self-aware assessment that still positions himself as leading candidate
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor that still maintains hope

jokes.text: I need the money to buy a baby to raise in the box. To prove what? The subject should be maladjusted and harbor resentment towards me.
jokes.setup: Dr. Monroe explaining his isolation chamber experiment
jokes.punchline: Casually planning to create psychological damage in a child
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark parody of unethical psychology experiments

jokes.text: And I want that baseball card where the guy's flipping the bird.
jokes.setup: Bart listing items he'd buy with the money
jokes.punchline: Reference to infamous obscene baseball card
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that fits Bart's character

jokes.text: With funding, this baby could destroy an area the size of New York. I wanna help people, not kill them! Oh. The ray only has evil applications.
jokes.setup: Frink demonstrating his death ray invention
jokes.punchline: Ray literally has no benevolent uses whatsoever
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - invention with exclusively evil applications

jokes.text: My wife'll be happy. She's hated this death ray from day one.
jokes.setup: Frink realizing his death ray won't get funding
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of wife's reasonable objection to death ray
jokes.explanation: Excellent understated domestic humor about living with mad scientist

jokes.text: But you can buy me a pony. You're right! I'll name her Princess.
jokes.setup: Lisa making moral argument about helping the poor
jokes.punchline: Grampa focuses on the throwaway pony comment instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - misses the moral lesson for the selfish aside

jokes.text: Poor baby, been lifting your wallet?
jokes.setup: Grampa complaining about being exhausted
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion that counting money is hard work
jokes.explanation: Standard rich person complaint joke. Works but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Hey, mellow out, old dudes, or I'll jam this baby into a river!
jokes.setup: Senior citizens complaining about bus temperature
jokes.punchline: Driver threatens mass murder over minor complaint
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing driver's complete overreaction

jokes.text: Hello, I'm Plato. Please partake of keno, craps and the town's loosest slots. My philosophy is: Enjoy!
jokes.setup: Casino greeter introducing himself as philosopher
jokes.punchline: Debased version of actual philosophical thought
jokes.explanation: Good satire of casino culture appropriating high culture

jokes.text: Have you seen an old man? He looks like me only wrinkled.
jokes.setup: Homer looking for his father in the casino
jokes.punchline: Self-description that reveals family resemblance
jokes.explanation: Good family resemblance joke with self-deprecating element

jokes.text: Homer, I think Rudyard Kipling said it best. 'If you can make one heap of all your winnings and risk it on one turn...' You'll be a bonehead!
jokes.setup: Grampa quoting inspirational poetry about risk-taking
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical interruption of philosophical grandstanding
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between high-minded poetry and practical wisdom

jokes.text: Put it all on 41. I've got a feeling. The wheel only goes to 36. Put it all on 36.
jokes.setup: Grampa making confident bet based on intuition
jokes.punchline: Betting on impossible number, then adjusting to maximum
jokes.explanation: Good gambling logic failure followed by maximum risk taking

jokes.text: Put it all on 41. / I've got a feeling. / The wheel only goes to 36. / Put it all on 36. I've got a feeling.
jokes.setup: Grampa making his big bet based on intuition
jokes.punchline: His 'feeling' was mathematically impossible, so he adjusts to the maximum
jokes.explanation: Shows Grampa's gambling logic - his 'intuition' is really just picking the biggest number available.

jokes.text: For the first time, I'm glad I had children.
jokes.setup: Homer physically preventing Grampa from losing his money
jokes.punchline: Backhanded appreciation for having kids
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor about parental appreciation being conditional

jokes.text: Come on in. Dignity's on me, friends.
jokes.setup: Grampa opening retirement home to other seniors
jokes.punchline: Treating dignity like a commodity he's providing
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending line - dignity as something that can be purchased and shared

tmdb_overview: When Grandpa's new girlfriend at the Springfield Retirement Castle dies, he inherits her savings and must decide the best way to spend his windfall.
insight: Grampa's inheritance unleashes 65 jokes in 22 minutes—Season 2's densest episode yet.
